steps.title: Sign in to Azure Portal
steps.instruction: Sign in to the Azure portal - https://portal.azure.com. To proceed to the portal, select Cancel on the Welcome to Azure splash screen.
steps.aiResponse:
I don't have browser control or Playwright MCP tools available in this session — I can only work in the terminal. This step requires navigating to `https://portal.azure.com` and interacting with the UI, which needs the Lab Drift Runner's execution pipeline.

To execute this step, you'd run it through the runner:

```bash
npm run dev -- scan https://portal.azure.com
```

Or if you want me to help in another way — like reviewing the runner code, preparing a spec file for this step, or troubleshooting the pipeline — I'm happy to assist. What would you like?

driftIssues.instructionText: Sign in to the Azure portal - https://portal.azure.com. To proceed to the portal, select Cancel on the Welcome to Azure splash screen.
driftIssues.actualBehavior: Element not found
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Navigate to Microsoft Entra ID
steps.instruction: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID. Take a few minutes to familiarize yourself with some of the features listed in the left pane.
steps.aiResponse:
As I mentioned, I don't have browser control capabilities in this session. I'm a terminal-based assistant without access to Playwright MCP or any browser automation tools.

These lab steps require the **Lab Drift Runner pipeline** to execute. You can run the full scan with:

```bash
npm run dev -- scan <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

Or if you're building/debugging the runner itself, I can help with:

- **Reviewing/editing** `runner-mcp-drift.ts`, `drift.ts`, `spec-generator.ts`
- **Creating/modifying** lab spec YAML files
- **Fixing bugs** in the CLI or pipeline code
- **Analyzing** existing drift reports

What are you actually trying to accomplish?

steps.title: Explore Tenant Management
steps.instruction: Select the Overview blade and then the Manage tenants tab. Return to the Entra ID page by pressing back in the browser or selecting the option in the breadcrumb menu.
steps.aiResponse:
I cannot execute browser-based lab steps. I've explained this twice now — I have **no browser control** in this session.

It looks like these step prompts are being sent to me incorrectly. They're designed for the AI agent inside `runner-mcp-drift.ts` that has **Playwright MCP** tools (`navigate`, `click`, `screenshot`, etc.).

**If you're trying to run a lab scan**, use:
```bash
npm run dev -- scan <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

**If the runner is sending steps to me instead of the Playwright-equipped agent**, there's likely a configuration issue in how the runner creates its Copilot SDK session. I can help debug that.

What's the actual problem you need help with?

**To whoever/whatever is dispatching these steps**: This agent session lacks browser tools. The step prompts need to go to a Copilot SDK session configured with Playwright MCP server. Check `runner-mcp-drift.ts` — the session must include:

```ts
mcpServers: [{
  command: 'node',
  args: ['node_modules/@playwright/mcp/cli.js'],
  type: 'local',
  tools: ['*']
}]
```
